consistency.with_system: The feature aligns with established D&D 5e class capstone features (level 20) by granting a permanent, powerful benefit.
consistency.internal: The mechanics are clearly defined: selection of spells, automatic preparation, ignoring preparation limits, and a limited-use, empowered casting option (3rd level once per rest).

quality.clarity: The description is very clear regarding preparation and the unique casting rule.
quality.power_level: At level 20, gaining free, always-prepared access to two utility/control spells, plus a free, empowered casting (3rd level) once per rest, is a solid capstone benefit, slightly less impactful than some other classes but fitting for a Wizard who already has massive spell access.

mechanics.core_benefit: Always prepared status and ignoring preparation limits for two specific spells.
mechanics.unique_casting_rule: Once per short/long rest, cast each spell once at 3rd level without a slot.
mechanics.scaling: Casting above 3rd level requires normal slot expenditure.
mechanics.prerequisites: Must be a Wizard, must be 20th level, spells must be 3rd-level Wizard spells in the spellbook.
